pages.raw_text:
The Structural Building Trades
alliance of Spokane today gave the
Press the following official version
of It's side of the recent trouble with
The Washington Water Power Co.
The statement is In the nature of a
reply to a colored account of the
trouble published in the Spokesman
Review September 20. After sum-
marking that story the statement
of the alliance continues as fol-
lows.


while the story does not war
rant any attention or contradiction
on the part of organized labor. we
believe that the public as a whole
is interested in all the struggles
between organized capital and Or
ganized labor, and as the pending
controversy may lead to further
complications in the future, the
unions deem 1t proper that their
side Of the trouble should be heard.


There has been storm cloud
lowering between the company and
organized labor for some time. The
unions hoped that the struggle
might be averted for be 1t remem-
bered that the union men of Spo-
kane are sober, Industrious citizens.
engaged In building home and
raising a family and not band of
ranting agitators.


About a year ago when the
business agent of the Stonemasons
called upon Mr.. Huntington, man.
ager or the w. w. p. Co., then
engaged In building his private
residence, and asked him IL he
would not patronize the union, and
employ some union stonemasons
the business agent was fatty told
that Mr.. Huntingdon would hire who
he pleased, union or non-union mer
and would not be dictated to by the
union.
